entry.title: Serve Single Page Applications using Spring Boot - React, Vue, anything #react #vuejs #spring #java

media:group.media:title: Serve Single Page Applications using Spring Boot - React, Vue, anything #react #vuejs #spring #java
media:group.media:description:
In this video, we serve multiple single page applications from a single spring boot backend. For single page application, any framework or library of choice can be used - react, vuejs, angular, svelte. We will address different problems we face while serving single page application from spring boot, related to routing, assets location and security. We will also see how to incorporate single page build process into the spring boot app's build process and how to enable rapid development in watch mode.
Some useful links: 1. Repository - https://github.com/himanshu-pareek/spring-spa 2. Securing Single Page Applications with Spring Security 🔐 - https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLbk-lC-jmHejQqn3ZzH7fuczWJiddjFuz 3. MFA in Spring Security 7 - Multifactor Authentication (MFA) in Spring Security 7 4. gradle-node-plugin - https://github.com/node-gradle/gradle-node-plugin 5. Tic-Tac-Toe Tutorial in React - https://react.dev/learn/tutorial-tic-tac-toe 6. Vue.js Quick Start - https://vuejs.org/guide/quick-start.html 7. Vite - https://vite.dev/guide/cli 8. npm-watch - https://github.com/M-Zuber/npm-watch
#spring #springboot #gradle #programming #java #nodejs #reactjs #vuejs #vite #npm #webdevelopment #security #springsecurity #tutorial

media:group.media:title: Multifactor Authentication (MFA) in Spring Security 7 #springsecurity #multifactorauthentication
media:group.media:description:
In this video, we will look at the Multi Factor Authentication using Spring Security introduced in Version 7. We will look at the following features of it: - Application Level MFA - MFA configurations at endpoint level - Time-bound MFA - User-centric MFA
You can find the code in the following GitHub Repository: - https://github.com/himanshu-pareek/spring-security-7-features/tree/main/mfa
Please fine the below useful resources: - Multifactor Authentication (OWASP) - https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Multifactor_Authentication_Cheat_Sheet.html - What's new in Spring Security 7.0 - https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/whats-new.html - Spring Security Release Notes - https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-security/releases - Multifactor Authentication (Spring Security) - https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/servlet/authentication/mfa.html
#springsecurity #multifactorauthentication #spring #security #java #tutorial
00:00 Introduction 03:50 Initializing Demo Project 07:45 Enable App-level MFA 10:11 AuthorizationManagerFactory 13:00 Selective MFA 17:20 Time-bound MFA 25:48 Programmatic MFA 32:55 User-level MFA Configuration 37:32 Other configurations

entry.title: 📒 Markdown Notes Project using Spring Boot and Flexmark - roadmap.sh #spring #roadmap #markdown

media:group.media:title: 📒 Markdown Notes Project using Spring Boot and Flexmark - roadmap.sh #spring #roadmap #markdown
media:group.media:description:
In this video, we will create a project - Markdown Notes taking Application - which is listed on roadmap.sh website. We will start with an empty Spring Boot Project and will add the features to upload notes written markdown language, to list the uploaded notes and to render uploaded notes in html. In order to convert the markdown to HTML, we will use flexmark library. Please find the below resources:
🧑‍💻 Github Repository - https://github.com/himanshu-pareek/roadmapsh-backend-projects/tree/main/markdown-notes 📋 Project Requirements - https://roadmap.sh/projects/markdown-note-taking-app 📁 How to upload files in Spring Boot - https://spring.io/guides/gs/uploading-files 📝 flexmark-java - https://github.com/vsch/flexmark-java 📄 commonmark javadoc (flexmark exposes / uses the same APIs) - https://www.javadoc.io/doc/org.commonmark/commonmark/latest/index.html 🍵 flexmark-java maven repository - https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/com.vladsch.flexmark/flexmark/0.64.8
#roadmap.sh #markdown #flexmark #commonmark #spring #springboot #java #project #proxy #api #html #notes

entry.title: Caching Proxy Project using Spring Data Redis - roadmap.sh #spring #redis #roadmap

media:group.media:title: Caching Proxy Project using Spring Data Redis - roadmap.sh #spring #redis #roadmap
media:group.media:description:
In this video, we will create a project - Caching Proxy - which is listed on roadmap.sh website. We will start with a simple proxy service without any caching, then we will implement caching using in memory map and then we will use Redis Cache to implement the caching proxy service. Please fine the below resources:
Github Repository - https://github.com/himanshu-pareek/roadmapsh-backend-projects/tree/main/caching-proxy Project Requirements - https://roadmap.sh/projects/caching-server Spring Data Redis - https://docs.spring.io/spring-data/redis/reference/ JSONPlaceholder Website - https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/
#roadmap.sh #cache #redis #spring #springboot #java #project #proxy #api #http #httpclient
00:00 What are we building 02:58 Project Setup 05:41 Build Direct Http Proxy 25:33 InMemory Caching Proxy 44:41 Redis Caching Proxy

entry.title: Todo Management Project using Spring Security JdbcUserDetailsService - Store users and tokens in db

media:group.media:title: Todo Management Project using Spring Security JdbcUserDetailsService - Store users and tokens in db
media:group.media:description:
In this video, we will take a look at the architecture of Todo API project. We will learn how to create custom authentication filters in Spring project to authenticate the user using Bearer Token. We will also see how we can use Database to manage users using Spring Security Jdbc User Details Manager. Please find the resources below:
Github Repository - https://github.com/himanshu-pareek/roadmapsh-backend-projects/tree/main/todo-list-api Project Requirements - https://roadmap.sh/projects/todo-list-api Spring Boot - https://docs.spring.io/spring-boot/index.html Spring Security - https://spring.io/projects/spring-security JdbcUserDetailsManager - https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/servlet/authentication/passwords/jdbc.html
#roadmap.sh #blog #spring #springboot #postgresqltutorial #postgresql #java #project #fullstack #postgres #todos #security #spring-security #filter

media:group.media:title: Weather API Wrapper Project - #redis #roadmap #roadmapshprojects #spring #caching
media:group.media:description:
In this video, we will create the Weather API Wrapper project which is listed on roadmap.sh website. We will call external weather service to get weather forecast and will store that in cache (Redis Server). Please find the resources below:
Github Repository - https://github.com/himanshu-pareek/roadmapsh-backend-projects/tree/main/weather_api Project Requirements - https://roadmap.sh/projects/weather-api-wrapper-service Spring Boot - https://docs.spring.io/spring-boot/index.html Redis - https://redis.io/ Redis image at Dockerhub - https://hub.docker.com/_/redis Spring Data Redis - https://spring.io/projects/spring-data-redis RestClient in Spring - https://docs.spring.io/spring-framework/reference/integration/rest-clients.html#rest-restclient
#roadmap.sh #blog #spring #springboot #thymeleaf #postgres #postgresql #tailwindcss #tailwind #java #html #css #javascript #ttcli #project #fullstack

entry.title: Personal Blog Project - 2 - TTCLI - Spring Boot + Thymeleaf + Postgres + Tailwind CSS (Node + NPM)

media:group.media:title: Personal Blog Project - 2 - TTCLI - Spring Boot + Thymeleaf + Postgres + Tailwind CSS (Node + NPM)
media:group.media:description:
In this video, we create Entity, Repository and Service to store Articles into the database for the Personal Blog Project. We then create a class to Initialize the database using fake article titles and content. We use flyway migration tool to create database table and then we create some dummy articles. Please find below useful links:
Github Repository - https://github.com/himanshu-pareek/roadmapsh-backend-projects/tree/main/personal-blog Project Requirements - https://roadmap.sh/projects/personal-blog TTCLI - https://github.com/wimdeblauwe/ttcli Spring Boot - https://docs.spring.io/spring-boot/index.html Thymeleaf - https://www.thymeleaf.org/ Postgres Database - https://www.postgresql.org/ Tailwind CSS - https://tailwindcss.com/ Java Faker - https://github.com/DiUS/java-faker Flyway - https://www.red-gate.com/products/flyway/community/
#roadmap.sh #blog #spring #springboot #thymeleaf #postgres #postgresql #tailwindcss #tailwind #java #html #css #javascript #ttcli #project #fullstack

entry.title: 🚢 Containerize Your Spring Boot App 🍃 with Buildpacks | No Dockerfile Needed! 🤔

media:group.media:title: 🚢 Containerize Your Spring Boot App 🍃 with Buildpacks | No Dockerfile Needed! 🤔
media:group.media:description:
Learn how to effortlessly create a Docker image for your Spring Boot application using Buildpacks — no Dockerfile required! In this step-by-step tutorial, we dive into the power of Buildpacks to simplify the containerization process for your Spring Boot applications. You'll also discover how Spring Boot automatically optimizes your Docker images using layered JARs, allowing for efficient caching and faster builds.
📌 Topics covered: Understanding layered Docker images and layered JAR for optimal performance Creating a Docker image for a Spring Boot app using Buildpacks without a Dockerfile Best practices for Dockerizing Spring Boot applications Update gradle / maven build system to configure creation of Docker image By the end of this video, you'll have a fully containerized Spring Boot app with a lightweight, layered Docker image—ready for seamless deployment and quick updates!
Resources: Blogs: https://blog.javarush.dev/creating-docker-image-of-spring-boot-application-using-buildpacks
https://dev.to/himanshu-pareek/creating-docker-image-of-spring-boot-application-using-buildpacks-5dbo
Buildpacks: https://buildpacks.io/ Container Images - Spring Boot: https://docs.spring.io/spring-boot/reference/packaging/container-images/index.html
#spring #springboot #docker #buildpacks #gradle #maven #tutorial #coding #springboottutorial #container #layeredjar #layeredimage
